description_long: This is a circular stone water well with a thick, weathered stone wall forming the main body. The well is topped by a simple wooden frame, constructed from rough-hewn timber beams, which arches over the opening but lacks any pulley or bucket mechanism. The stonework is irregular, with visible moss and discoloration, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The interior of the well is open and appears deep, with no visible water surface or cover. The structure is robust and utilitarian, with no decorative elements or protective railing. The overall appearance is rustic, evoking traditional wells found in rural or historical urban settings.
description: A rustic stone water well with a thick circular wall and a simple wooden frame above, lacking any pulley or cover. The stone is weathered and mossy, giving it an aged, utilitarian look.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thick stone wall and the wooden frame's vertical post, with the well's open interior visible.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the full width of the wooden frame and the circular stone opening, with the well's interior clearly visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the stone wall and the wooden frame's vertical post from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the wooden frame's horizontal beam and the stone well's open top, with the interior stonework visible.
